titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the David Ottenstein Photographs of Iowa
titlestmt.author: by Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 9139870.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday December 29, 2017 at 22:02

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Ink jet print photographs that document the transformation of the Iowa countryside from traditional family farms to industrial farmsteads, as well as the growth of agricultural industries in the American Midwest, 2004-2010. The images document traditional farmhouses, barns, silos, corn cribs, sheds and other farm buildings. They detail the physical changes of the natural and built landscape that accompany the social and economic trends at the turn of the of twenty-first century in the American Midwest. The collection includes some images of Illinois.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition note
acqinfo.p: Purchased from David Ottenstein Photography on Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana and Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2007-2010.

accessrestrict.head: Information about Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Ownership Copyright
userestrict.p: The David Ottenstein Photographs of Iowa collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Cite As
prefercite.p: David Ottenstein Photographs of Iowa. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Notes
processinfo.p: This guide derives from lists of print titles and dates provided by David Ottenstein.

bioghist.head: David Ottenstein (born 1960)
bioghist.p: David Ottenstein graduated from Yale College in 1982 with a degree in American Studies and a concentration in Photography. Ottenstein has worked as a free-lance photographer based in New Haven, Connecticut.

scopecontent.head: Description of the Papers
scopecontent.p: Ink jet print photographs that document the transformation of the Iowa countryside from traditional family farms to industrial farmsteads, as well as the growth of agricultural industries in the American Midwest, 2004-2010. The images document traditional farmhouses, barns, silos, corn cribs, sheds and other farm buildings. They detail the physical changes of the natural and built landscape that accompany the social and economic trends at the turn of the of twenty-first century in the American Midwest. The collection includes some images of Illinois.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into one series: I. Photographs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Series I is organized into two subseries: 11" X 17" Prints and 16" X 20" Prints

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically by capture date and then alphabetically by title.

did.unittitle: Cemetery - Elkport Lutheran Church, Elkport, Iowa
Edge of Town, La Motte, Iowa
View South from B B Farms
Corn Field West of Taintor, Iowa
Corn Crib South of Ladora, Iowa
Silos and Barn Near West Point
Broadway and High Street, Toledo, Ohio
